process_doctrine.bug_to_regression_rule: Every real smoke failure becomes automated regression test, diagnostic/coverage test, documented limitation, or backlog item.
complex_backend_quality_gate: Identify the exact failing, missing, or underperforming user path.
Add a failing regression test, diagnostic test, or coverage test where practical.
Confirm the test fails or exposes the gap before implementation.
Apply the smallest safe implementation change.
Re-run the original manual/browser smoke path.
process_doctrine.provider_ai_rule: Provider may propose. Backend validates. User sees only approved output.

risk_model.high: workout generation, catalog selection/scoring, state/persistence, nutrition targets, provider output, recommendations: diagnostic first, failing/coverage test, narrow patch, regression validation, original smoke, Linux/browser smoke, project memory, Architecture acceptance
risk_model.low: docs/typo/isolated helper: normal patch and focused validation
risk_model.medium: deterministic service or bounded integration: light diagnostic, focused test, narrow patch, regression validation, smoke if user-visible

stop_conditions: same bug survives two implementation patches
tests pass but browser smoke fails
Linux smoke fails after Windows green
candidate pools or data shape are unclear
implementation requires broader scope than approved
patch fails to apply because of drift and next step is not obvious
implementation crosses into deferred v2 scope
process_doctrine.v1_v2_scope_rule: Architecture defines v1 acceptance and deferred v2 scope before implementation when a feature could expand.

snapshot_ownership_main_acceptance_artif.canonical_accepted_snapshots: Created from main after Architecture acceptance/merge and named with the accepted main commit hash.
snapshot_ownership_main_acceptance_artif.feature_snapshots: Optional implementation artifacts; not canonical unless explicitly designated.

project_memory_warning_review_v1.goal: Resolve current/actionable stale project-memory state and document remaining warnings as accepted historical/archive noise.

linux: Runtime/smoke environment at ~/projects/fitness-ai-platform. Pull feature or main branches after push/merge and run Linux validation when runtime-relevant.
windows: Primary development workstation at C:\projects\fitness_ai. Use Windows for patch application, validation, explicit staging, commit, push, and feature snapshots.

status.active_backend_milestone: Recovery Intelligence v2 Model Contract v1

team_routing_contract.project_memory_note: Project Memory / All Future Agents is not a visible team lane; it is a repo continuity concern every team must respect.

workflow_rules.apply_raw_patches_from_repo_as: Apply raw patches from the repo root using git apply --check ..\<patch>.patch then git apply ..\<patch>.patch.
workflow_rules.long_handoffs_in_code_blocks: Long handoffs must be delivered as one copy/paste-ready code block.
workflow_rules.no_broad_formatters_for_docs_only: For docs-only milestones, do not run broad formatters that can rewrite unrelated application files.
workflow_rules.no_git_add_dot: Do not use git add .; stage explicit files only.
workflow_rules.no_repo_wide_mutating_formatters_for_fea: Use targeted Ruff/Black on touched files only. Use whole-repo checks in non-mutating mode when appropriate.
workflow_rules.phase_separated_delivery: Use separate phases: preflight/branch, implement/apply, validate, review diff, stage only, commit only, push only, snapshot, Linux pull/smoke.
workflow_rules.push_separate_phase: Push must be its own separate phase; do not combine commit and push.
workflow_rules.run_apply_scripts_from_repo_as: Run temporary apply scripts from the repo root using python ..\<script>.py.
workflow_rules.temporary_apply_artifacts_outside_repo: Temporary patches and apply scripts stay outside the repo.
